feat: skip reason logging + resolution priority swap

Skip reasons:
- Every SKIP now records WHY: SCORE_TOO_LOW, POSITION_LIMIT_N/M,
  PAUSED_BY_USER, DEAD_HOUR_N, DUPLICATE_POSITION, IN_FLIGHT,
  RISK_LIMIT_$X+$Y>$Z, EXEC_FAILED
- Added skipReason column to decisions CSV (after action column)
- Dashboard score feed shows skip reason in orange text

Resolution priority (fixes wrong resolution on tight margins):
- Method 1 (PRIMARY): CLOB API — Polymarket's own winner field
- Method 2: Book price (mid >= 0.95 or <= 0.05)
- Method 3 (FALLBACK): Binance spot vs strike (only after 15s delay)
- Method 4: 30-min timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add trust system v2: blind reviews, Bayesian engine, dimensions, stake vouching

Phase 1: Mutual blind rating protocol (reviews hidden until both submit or 7 days),
Bayesian reputation engine with recency decay and platform prior, exchange-type tagging.

Phase 2: 5-dimension structured ratings (reliability, communication, accuracy,
generosity, community) with exchange-type-aware prompts, stake-weighted vouching
(reputation risk/reward, 5-vouch limit), reviewer credibility coefficients.

New files: migration, reveal-reviews cron, recalc-credibility cron,
DimensionRatingInput component, alert-dialog UI component.

- PRs #51-#53 documented: Audits UI, SearchPage, ToneTrend chart

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: remove all CMake configs before Tesseract build

libtiff, libjpeg-turbo, and leptonica all export CMake target
configs with transitive dependencies (ZLIB::ZLIB, JPEG::JPEG,
CMath::CMath) that fail to resolve during Tesseract's configure.
Remove the entire cmake/ directory from the sysroot before building
Tesseract — it falls back to pkg-config which handles Requires.private
correctly, and FFmpeg only uses pkg-config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove self-signed certificate scripts and signing steps from release
pipeline (not effective for SmartScreen). Update icon to simple "K"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add JWT authentication with Argon2 password hashing

- POST /api/v1/auth/signup — register, returns access + refresh tokens
- POST /api/v1/auth/login  — validate credentials, returns tokens
- POST /api/v1/auth/refresh — exchange refresh token for new access token
- GET /api/v1/users now requires a valid Bearer access token

Implementation details:
- AuthService: Argon2 hashing in spawn_blocking, jsonwebtoken for JWTs
- AuthenticatedUser extractor: validates Bearer token on protected routes
- UserResponse: password-free response type used in all API responses
- AppConfig: jwt_secret, jwt_access_expiration_secs, jwt_refresh_expiration_secs
- AppError::InvalidToken variant maps to 401
- Filled in users migration (CREATE TABLE + unique index on email)
- Switch sqlx from runtime-async-std to runtime-tokio-native-tls (correct
  for a Tokio-based app; also fixes spawn_blocking in #[sqlx::test] tests)

Tests:
- 14 unit tests in services/auth.rs (no DB required)
- 14 integration tests in api/auth.rs via #[sqlx::test] (isolated DB per test)

commit.message:
fix(youtube-listener-innertube): implement source-manager integration

The innertube listener was not discovering any YouTube sources because the
periodicSync method was just a stub. This commit implements the missing
functionality:

- Added smClient field to Manager to query source-manager
- Implemented periodicSync to fetch active YouTube sources
- Implemented syncSources to start discovery for each active source
- Updated main.go to pass source-manager client to NewManager

This enables the innertube listener to:
1. Query source-manager every 30s for active YouTube sources
2. Start async discovery for new channels
3. Begin polling live streams when discovered

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test(flights): add award flight tests and extract wizard payload builder

a month because there were zero tests for the award flight submit path
in either intake component.

  testable function

feat: initial release — 30+ AI provider quota monitor

Cross-platform system tray app (Windows/macOS/Linux) built with Tauri 2 + React 19.

Providers (30+):
- Local file: Claude Code, Cursor, JetBrains AI, Continue.dev, Zed, Aider
- CLI exec: Kiro
- API key: OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Mistral, Groq, Perplexity, Kimi, Together, Fireworks, Tabnine, Cody
- OAuth: GitHub Copilot, Gemini, Vertex AI
- Browser cookie: Codex, Windsurf, Augment, Amp, Bolt, v0, Lovable, Droid
- OS Keychain: z.ai, Antigravity

Features:
- Cross-platform Chrome cookie decrypt (DPAPI/AES-GCM on Windows, Keychain+AES-CBC on macOS, Linux)
- Per-provider polling intervals (30s local, 5min API)
- OS-native keychain for API key storage
- Dark theme system tray popup UI

commit.message:
chore: migrate library packages to peerDependencies for @goodie-ts/core (#95)

Move @goodie-ts/core from dependencies to peerDependencies (>=1.0.0)
in all 9 library packages (cache, logging, resilience, health, hono,
kysely, testing, events, scheduler). Build-time tools (cli, vite-plugin,
transformer) keep regular dependencies. This prevents duplicate copies
of core causing class identity mismatches at runtime.

commit.message:
Broaden Zoom name matching — match on any shared first/last name

The previous matching was too strict, requiring both first AND last
names to match. Now any single shared name part (first or last) is
enough for a match. Also added edit distance tolerance (1 typo) and
prefix matching for shorter name forms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: pivot to day trading — all 6 phases implemented

Phase 1 — Strategy overhaul:
- Disabled swing strategies (trend_following, mean_reversion, momentum)
- Added Opening Range Breakout (ORB) and VWAP Bounce strategies
- Day trade: 3% risk, 2.5 ATR take-profit, 0.75 ATR trailing stop
- Session windows: prime hours 9:30-11:30 and 14:30-16:00

Phase 2 — Instrument selection:
- Focused day-trade watchlist: US500, US100, AAPL, NVDA, TSLA, BTCUSD, ETHUSD, GOLD
- Separate day_trade and day_trade_ibkr sections in watchlist.yaml

Phase 3 — Monitor as primary engine:
- 2-minute scan interval (was 5)
- Opening range capture at 9:45 AM ET
- Session tracking with daily P&L, win/loss, consecutive losses
- Daily gain target ($50) and loss limit ($30) auto-stop
- Crypto 24/7 monitoring (--crypto-only mode)
- EOD auto-close at 3:55 PM ET (stocks only, crypto stays open)

Phase 4 — Execution precision:
- Time-based exits: close after 60 min if < 0.3 ATR profit
- Spread tracking and 0.5% spread filter
- Consecutive loss circuit breaker (3 losses → 30 min cooldown)

Phase 5 — Risk management:
- Correlation checking (same group + direction → 50% size reduction)
- Max daily exposure 3x balance
- Per-instrument daily loss limit ($15)
- Composite risk hard block lowered to 6 (was 7)
- Increased behavioral risk weight for day trading

Phase 6 — Reporting:
- Enhanced trade journal: setup_type, duration, session_window, exit_type
- Daily recap: W/L breakdown, setup performance, session analysis
- Weekly digest: best/worst day, streak tracking, setup comparison
- /journal Telegram command for last 5 trades

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
